title: [Feature]: Add edge spool-and-forward mode for disconnected operation
problem: Edge and deployed environments may experience intermittent network availability, dependency outages, or restricted connectivity. nats-sinks currently relies on JetStream redelivery and destination availability, but it does not provide a local encrypted spool that can preserve normalized events for later durable forwarding when a destination is temporarily unavailable.
proposal: Research and design an optional edge spool-and-forward mode with encrypted local storage, bounded disk usage, deterministic idempotency, priority-aware draining, safe cleanup, and explicit operator controls. The mode should remain optional and should not weaken the core commit-then-acknowledge invariant.
users: Operators in disconnected or degraded networks, platform engineers, field-support teams, mission-support integrators, and maintainers designing reliable edge deployments.
delivery_semantics: The ACK boundary must be explicit. If local spool is configured as the durable destination for a phase, ACK may occur only after the event is safely committed to the encrypted spool. Forwarding from spool to a final sink is a second at-least-once workflow that must preserve idempotency and recovery behavior.
security: Spool files must be encrypted when enabled, bounded by size and count, stored under a validated base directory, protected from path traversal, and excluded from logs. Operators must understand that local disk custody has different risk than database or object storage custody.
acceptance: A design document defines spool durability, ACK boundary, replay behavior, and failure modes.
Configuration supports explicit opt-in and safe defaults.
Spool storage is bounded, path-safe, and compatible with payload encryption.
Replay preserves idempotency keys and metadata.
Tests cover crash recovery, duplicate replay, full spool behavior, and cleanup.
tests: Begin with design tests for path validation and metadata serialization. If implemented, add unit and integration tests for encrypted spool writes, replay, duplicate handling, full-disk simulation where practical, priority-aware draining, and graceful shutdown.
documentation: Update docs/operations.md, docs/security.md, docs/file-sink.md, docs/idempotency.md, docs/use-cases, ROADMAP.md, and CHANGELOG.md with diagrams and operator guidance.
closeout: Close after the release containing design or implementation is published. Include release tag, ACK boundary decision, tests run, and limitations.
